summary: Run autonomous browser QA against code changes, suites, bug regressions, and release flows, then return reproducible failure evidence.

inputs.name: credential_strategy
inputs.description: How login credentials are provided to browser automation.

inputs.name: journey_scope
inputs.description: Suite, file, tag, release checklist, CSV, changed route, or ordered list of user flows to execute.

inputs.name: evidence_policy
inputs.description: Whether to run headed, capture screenshots/GIFs, and include console or network observations.

outputs.name: qa_report
outputs.description: Pass/fail/error/manual counts, scoped coverage summary, and residual risks.

outputs.name: reproduction_steps
outputs.description: Deterministic repro steps for each failing journey.

outputs.name: failure_artifacts
outputs.description: Screenshot, GIF, video, trace, or HTML report references for failed browser states.

workflow_steps: Verify a Browser Use QA harness or identify the repo's equivalent browser automation setup.
Choose the narrowest useful scope from suite, file, tag, release checklist, CSV, or changed code.
Generate focused markdown test cases when existing coverage does not exercise the requested flow.
Run headed first for new or flaky flows, then parallelize stable suites.
Separate product bugs from harness failures and return evidence-backed findings.

verification.smoke_test: Run one headed Browser Use smoke flow against a local or staging URL and verify the final report includes counts, repro steps, and artifact paths for failures.
